light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Fair to good trout fishing conditions persist on the Toccoa River, boosted by recent stocking activity. Expect stocked trout to respond well to eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs in comfortable feeding lanes.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning offers the best fishing window due to stable light and pressure conditions combined with recent stocking effects, optimizing trout feeding behavior.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS data shows a flow of 227 cfs and gage height of 1.2 ft; water temperature and turbidity readings are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: The sky cover averages 30%, pressure is steady at 30.08 inHg with no significant trend, winds are light averaging 3.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and a slight rain chance of 0.1 inches is expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position near runs, seams, and structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes under moderate light. Employ a balanced nymph approach using smaller natural patterns and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracells for clean drifts and effective presentation.
